page_content: ENEE290 Introduction to Differential Equations and Linear Algebra for Engineers (4 Credits) First-order differential equations, matrices and systems of linear equations, ﬁnite-dimensional vector spaces, inner product spaces, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, linear differential equations of higher order, and systems of differential equations. This course covers important topics in mathematics for Electrical and Computer Engineers. Speciﬁcally, several topics are covered, including ﬁrst-order differential equations, matrices and systems of linear equations, ﬁnite-dimensional vector spaces, inner product spaces, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, linear differential equations of higher order, and systems of differential equations. Theoretical topics presented in the lectures will be reinforced by laboratory exercises. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in MATH141; and permission of ENGR- Electrical & Computer Engineering department. Restriction: Must be in one of the following programs (Engineering: Electrical; Engineering: Computer). Credit Only Granted for: ENEE290, MATH240, MATH246, or MATH461.
